title: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese

seo.title: How to Make Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese
easy Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese recipe
authentic Chinese Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese
lowCompetitionKeywords: Chinese Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese Recipe
h2Headings: What is Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a quintessential dish showcasing the delicate flavors of fresh fish. It highlights the natural sweetness and tender texture of the seafood, enhanced by a fragrant soy sauce dressing and aromatic ginger and green onions. This simple yet elegant preparation is a testament to Cantonese cuisine's emphasis on fresh ingredients and balanced tastes.

ingredients.notes: half cut into 2-inch pieces, half julienned

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Rinse the cleaned fish thoroughly under cold water, pat dry with paper towels. Make two or three diagonal slits on each side of the fish, about 1/2 inch deep. Rub the fish all over with Shaoxing wine and white pepper. Stuff some sliced ginger and 2-inch green onion pieces into the cavity and place a few pieces on top of the fish.

instructions.title: Set Up Steamer
instructions.text: Fill a large wok or steamer pot with water, ensuring it does not touch the steaming rack. Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat. Place a heatproof plate or platter suitable for steaming on the rack.

instructions.title: Steam the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place the prepared fish on the plate in the steamer. Cover tightly and steam for 8-12 minutes, depending on the thickness of the fish. The fish is done when the flesh turns opaque and flakes easily with a fork. Do not overcook.

instructions.title: Prepare Aromatic Garnish
instructions.text: While the fish is steaming, prepare the julienned ginger and green onions. In a small bowl, combine the light soy sauce and sesame oil. Set aside.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the fish is cooked, carefully remove the plate from the steamer. Discard the cooked ginger and green onion pieces from the fish and any accumulated liquid on the plate. This removes any fishy odors. Pour the soy sauce mixture evenly over the fish. Arrange the fresh julienned ginger and green onions generously over the fish.

instructions.title: Sizzle with Hot Oil
instructions.text: Heat the peanut or neutral oil in a small saucepan over high heat until it just begins to smoke. Carefully and quickly pour the sizzling hot oil over the julienned ginger and green onions on the fish. This step 'cooks' the aromatics and releases their fragrance. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

tips: Always use the freshest possible fish. For best results, buy the fish on the day you plan to cook it.
Do not overcook the fish. Overcooked steamed fish will be dry and tough. The cooking time varies greatly depending on the thickness of your fish.
The sizzling hot oil step is crucial for releasing the maximum aroma from the fresh ginger and green onions, giving the dish its signature fragrance.
article: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a beloved Chinese Seafood dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

3-4 tbsp Peanut oil or neutral oil
